symbols: massive stone arches — endurance and the passage of time
river flowing beneath — the ceaseless movement of time and commerce
hazy cityscape in distance — the atmospheric dissolution of earthly structures
twilight sky — the liminal hour between day and night
description: A watercolor view of Old London Bridge from beneath or alongside its massive stone arches. The bridge dominates the composition with its heavy piers and pointed Gothic arches. Through the arches, a hazy view of the distant London cityscape is visible, including what may be church towers and other bridges further along the Thames. Small figures and a boat are visible near the water's edge at right. The palette is predominantly warm amber and brown tones with a pale, luminous sky beyond. The atmosphere is misty and evocative, characteristic of Turner's handling of light on water.
